content:
Is Shared Sorrow = Half Sorrow?
In the past, people always have been living in communities and groups. There was this sense of collectivism. But, in the current modern age, why is the world at large more individualistic? Why do people don’t need each other anymore. There sure is collectivism out there but the count of such collective beliefs is gradually decreasing.
Technology answers most of our questions here. We dedicate plenty of our time and interactions to the current modern technology, eradicating the need of having something else to fill in our empty time.
Cons of Individualism #The individualism however, originates the sense of loneliness within ourselves. You see humans, by nature, are not meant to be surviving alone. The sense of strong life establishment we feel within groups and people justifies it. This could be one of the major flaw of opting the individualistic belief.
Beauty of Individualism #But this isn’t everything the individualistic belief offers us. Looking at the brighter side, we as a fish get a whole sea for ourselves. Allowing us to understand life the way we encounter it. Allowing us to decide freely from a range full of options. This could be the power of solitude.
The freedom from people allows us the freedom to spend our every vacant minute doing whatever we want, whenever we want
The people who benefit from solitude are the ones who wholeheartedly appreciate it. From their perspective, the fear of not being able to attain is never there. They have no one to compete. They drive their own self and thus they compete with their very set records. This very way of thinking is extremely powerful. This is because there’s no stopping for them. They just keep on progressing with no directional feedbacks.
Socialism #Not needing friends allows you to set your own boundaries. Instead of you fitting inside a social circle. The right fit of friends actually finds you. They build around you. This doesn’t mean you just cut them off, but instead you will have the mental authority to walk away from the wrong fit of friends. You help people who seek your help and when you need the help, you never expect it from anyone. This allows you to build a mental bubble around you that not only saves you from emotional disappointment but also adds to your ability to stand up by yourself. This way, even though there will be people helping you out. Your mental will always be ready to stand up by yourself never relying on the help of others in the first place.
Self Adaptation #Belonging requires us to adapt
People expects us in many regards to perform how they do, adapt how they do and think how they do. We are never free from the gaze of people. Those judging eyes will always be there and there is simply no escape from them. The more people we surround ourselves with, the more judging eyes are there. This also links to the Hawthorne’s effect1.
“A man can be himself only so long as he is alone; and if he does not love solitude, he will not love freedom; for it is only when he is alone that he is really free.”
– Arthur Schopenhauer, Essays and Aphorisms
True Sense of Solitude #Not needing people doesn’t imply not associating with people at all but it grants us the possibility to embrace solitude when desired. We can associate with people, befriend them and even develop intimacy, but with the freedom to walk away whenever we please, we can achieve the true self-happiness. ‍
Hawthorne’s effect
Individual modify an aspect of their behaviour in response to their awareness of being observed
Simply observing someone can change the way they perform a particular act. This can also relate to the dual nature of light discovered by the double-slit experiment. ↩︎
title: Joys of Not Needing People

content:
L’appetit vient en mangeant is a french proverb which means “appetite comes from eating”
It means that when you eat something, consume something, you only want more. The more you consume the more you want it.
Lets take eating for an example. There are times when we are not really hungry all but the thought of eating, having something to eat, that very act itself triggers us to eat and creates that sense of hunger inside of us, which maybe wasn’t at all in that moment.
How the Human Hunger System Work # There are three phases of digestion, the first of which is the Cephalic Phase Response. The other two are the Gastric and Intestinal phases of digestion.
The Cephalic Phase is triggered by thesight, smell, taste or even thought of food. During the cephalic phase gastric secretions begin, which include increasing salivation and the secretion of gastric acid, hormones, and insulin. The cephalic phase signals your body that food is coming and prepares your body to digest it. Some studies have found that triggering the cephalic phase can create appetite/hunger.
Relationship with Other Aspects of Life #This thing not only applies to food at all. It applies to just about everything we consume. The information we intake to the interests and habits we develop.
We would only want more the more we consume something.
That’s the so called nature of a human body that could be beyond our control
So, how do we presume it to be? Is it really a bad thing? or just a flaw we need to accept
No matter the answer, we can definitely levitate it to our benefit. Just think about the possibilities, the grooming potential, by just consuming the right content and establishing the hunger for it.
Who knows that hunger for the right thing is what we call "passion"
Establishing the hunger for the right thing can pave your way to glory
summary: The more you consume, the more you seek
title: Ever Increasing Appetite
